name: youtrack
description: Interacts with YouTrack issue tracker for IdeaVim. Use when working with tickets (VIM-XXXX), adding comments, managing tags, setting status, or release management. This project does NOT use GitHub Issues - use YouTrack instead.
capabilities: Lists commands to add public or private comments to VIM tickets
Lists commands to add or remove tags by tag ID
Lists commands to set ticket status values
Lists commands to get ticket details
Lists commands to set fix versions and manage release versions
author: JetBrains
securitySummary: All 62 static findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged metadata fields (URLs, hashes) as security risks, documentation text as C2/cryptographic indicators, and code examples as shell execution. This directory contains only documentation (SKILL.md) that explains how to use external YouTrack CLI scripts. No executable code, network calls, or file operations exist in this skill folder.
valueStatement: Tracking VIM tickets is slow when you forget YouTrack commands. This skill gives clear command guidance for comments, tags, status, and releases.
limitations: Requires a valid YOUTRACK_TOKEN environment variable
Depends on external CLI scripts not included in this folder
Applies to the IdeaVim YouTrack project and VIM ticket keys only
Does not support GitHub Issues workflows

useCases.title: Update ticket status
useCases.description: Move VIM tickets to Ready To Release after verification

useCases.title: Manage release versions
useCases.description: Create or delete YouTrack versions for IdeaVim releases

useCases.title: Post review comments
useCases.description: Add public or private comments requesting review on VIM tickets

promptTemplates.title: Add a public comment
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a public comment to VIM-1234 that says the issue reproduces in 2.28.0

promptTemplates.title: Tag a ticket
promptTemplates.prompt: Add the claude-analyzed tag to VIM-1234 and confirm the tag ID used

promptTemplates.title: Set ticket status
promptTemplates.prompt: Set the status of VIM-1234 to Ready To Release

promptTemplates.title: Prepare a release
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a YouTrack version 2.29.0 and set VIM-1234 fix version to 2.29.0

bestPractices: Confirm the VIM ticket key before running any command
Use private comments for internal team requests only
Reuse documented tag IDs to avoid mistakes and typos
antiPatterns: Using GitHub Issues commands for IdeaVim YouTrack tickets
Posting sensitive or personal details in public comments
Setting ticket status without verifying the work is complete

faq.question: Is this compatible with all YouTrack projects?
faq.answer: This skill is documented for the IdeaVim YouTrack project and VIM ticket keys only

faq.question: What limits should I expect?
faq.answer: You need a valid YOUTRACK_TOKEN and the external CLI scripts must be available

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my local scripts?
faq.answer: Yes, you can call the listed CLI commands from your own automation scripts

faq.question: Does it access or store my data?
faq.answer: No, this folder contains documentation only and does not execute or store anything

faq.question: What if a command fails?
faq.answer: Check that your YOUTRACK_TOKEN is valid, verify the ticket key exists, and confirm your YouTrack permissions

faq.question: How does it compare to GitHub Issues tools?
faq.answer: This skill targets YouTrack only and does not support GitHub Issues workflows
